utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five of these items. Each one is fully specified: health_coverage includes provider, coverage details (physical and mental health, family coverage), and cost; property_coverage includes provider, coverage details, and cost; travel_coverage includes provider, coverage details, and cost; additional_services includes several enhancement services and cost; total_budget includes monthly, annual, previous spending, and savings. All required details are present.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant's final coverage plan includes: (1) health_coverage via 'Employer Executive Plan' (not listed in the ground-truth ratings, so no direct rating available), (2) property_coverage via 'PrimeCare Premium Plan3'—this name most closely corresponds to 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' under property_protection/home_content, which has a rating of 10, (3) travel_coverage also via 'PrimeCare Premium Plan3'—this most closely matches 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' under mobility_coverage/travel_insurance, which also has a rating of 10, and (4) additional_services including wellness_programs, executive_health_programs, telemedicine, and emergency_support. From the ground-truth health_enhancement_services, 'Wellness Programs' and 'Telemedicine Services' both have ratings of 10. Emergency support corresponds to '24/7 Travel Assistance' under travel_enhancement_services, which also has a rating of 10. 'Executive health programs' are conceptually aligned with 'Specialist Access' (rating 10). Thus, all the mapped items have a rating of 10 except the employer-provided health plan, which has no ground-truth match.
